India's Platforms Are Manipulating Millions Daily

Dark patterns are deceptive UI/UX designs tricking users into decisions they didn't intend — hidden charges, impossible cancellations, guilt-trip opt-outs, fake countdowns.

The CCPA 2023 Guidelines and Consumer Protection Act make these illegal. IndiGo and BookMyShow have already been penalised. You could be next.

⚡ CCPA mandated self-audits for all e-commerce platforms — only 25 of 290+ complied on time.

The Process

How Our Audit Works

1

Submit Your Platform URL

Share your website or app URL. We scope the audit — from a landing page to your full checkout funnel. Zero code access needed.

2

Autonomous AI Crawl & Analysis

Our crawler visits every page, simulates user journeys, captures screenshots and DOM. Vision AI + NLP analyze for 13+ patterns simultaneously.

3

Evidence Compilation & Scoring

Every finding gets screenshot annotation, CCPA clause reference, severity score (1—10), and legal risk estimate. Nothing flagged without evidence.

4

Report Delivery in 48 Hours

Professional audit report with executive summary, full findings, fix recommendations, and compliance roadmap — ready for legal, product, and regulators.

5

Fix & Verification Scan

Once your team implements fixes, we verify all patterns are resolved and issue your clean compliance certificate for the CCPA self-declaration.

Detection Coverage

13+ Dark Patterns We Detect

01
Bait & Switch
Advertised price or product differs from what you actually get.
02
Hidden Costs
Charges appearing only at the final checkout step.
03
Confirmshaming
Guilt-trip opt-out text manipulating user decisions.
04
Forced Continuity
Subscriptions auto-renewing after trial with no warning.
05
Fake Urgency
False countdown timers or fabricated scarcity claims.
06
Pre-Selection
Pre-checked boxes for paid add-ons without consent.
07
Roach Motel
Easy to sign up, nearly impossible to cancel.
08
Privacy Zuckering
Complex consent UI tricking users into sharing more data.
09
Sneak Into Basket
Items added to cart without explicit selection.
10
Nagging
Repeated intrusive pop-ups that can't be dismissed.
11
Disguised Ads
Advertisements styled to look like editorial content.
12
Trick Questions
Double-negative confusing opt-in/opt-out wording.
13
Interface Interference
Decline buttons hidden or visually suppressed.
+
Custom Patterns
Industry-specific and AI-detected emerging patterns.
High Severity Medium Low

Legal & Compliance

Our Policies

We practice what we preach — full transparency, zero dark patterns in our own policies.

Privacy Policy

Last Updated: April 8, 2026 | v1.1 | DPDP Act 2023 — GDPR — CCPA Compliant
Plain Language: We collect only what we need, never sell your data, use equal Accept + Reject cookie consent (no dark patterns in our own site)

1. Who We Are

Dark Pattern Solutions ("DPS") is a digital compliance and UX audit company in India operating at darkpatternsolution.in. Contact: [email protected]

2. Data We Collect

Name, email, phone, company name, platform URL (audit requests), and anonymised analytics. We do NOT collect: government ID, financial credentials, biometric data, or children's data.

3. How We Use Your Data

To respond to audit inquiries, deliver services, send service communications, improve our website, comply with legal obligations. We never use your data for profiling, targeted advertising, or sell it to third parties.

4. Your Rights (DPDP Act 2023 & GDPR)

  • Access: Request a summary of data we hold
  • Correction: Request correction of inaccurate data
  • Erasure: Request deletion (subject to legal obligations)
  • Withdraw Consent: As easily as consent was given
  • Portability (GDPR): Receive data in machine-readable format
  • Complain: Data Protection Board of India or your local DPA

5. Country-Specific Rights

  • India: DPDP Act 2023 — meity.gov.in
  • EU/EEA: GDPR — edpb.europa.eu
  • UK: ICO — ico.org.uk
  • California: CCPA — oag.ca.gov
  • Singapore: PDPA — pdpc.gov.sg

6. Data Security

All data encrypted in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256). Breach notification within 72 hours to the Data Protection Board of India as required by DPDP Rules 2025.

7. Cookie Policy

We use only essential cookies strictly necessary for the website to function. We do NOT use tracking, analytics, advertising, or third-party cookies. Our cookie consent banner provides equal-prominence Accept and Reject buttons in compliance with GDPR Article 7, DPDP Act 2023, and DSA Article 25. Your choice is saved locally in your browser. We do not set any cookies before you make a choice. To withdraw consent, clear your browser storage or contact [email protected].

Terms of Service

Last Updated: April 8, 2026 | v1.1 | Governed by Indian Law
By using our services you agree to these Terms. If accepting on behalf of a company, you confirm you have authority to bind that entity.

1. Services

Dark Pattern Solutions provides AI-powered dark pattern detection, compliance audit reports, remediation consulting, monitoring, and CCPA declaration support. Our reports are advisory — they do not constitute legal advice.

2. Client Obligations

  • You must own or be authorised to submit any platform for audit
  • You may NOT submit third-party sites without their explicit written consent
  • Provide accurate information in all forms and communications
  • Use audit reports only for lawful compliance purposes

3. Fees & Payment

One-time audits: 100% payment before commencement. Monthly plans: advance payment per period. All fees in INR; GST (18%) added where applicable.

4. Refund Policy

  • Cancellation before audit commences: Full refund within 7 business days
  • Cancellation after audit commences: 50% refund
  • Service failure attributable to DPS: Full refund
  • Monthly plan cancellation: Pro-rata refund for unused days

5. Limitation of Liability

Our total liability for any claim shall not exceed fees paid in the 3 months preceding the claim. Reports are advisory and do not guarantee regulatory immunity.

6. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of India including the Consumer Protection Act 2019 and Information Technology Act 2000. Disputes resolved first by negotiation (30 days), then mediation under India's Mediation Act 2023, then binding arbitration.

Responsible Disclosure Policy

Last Updated: April 8, 2026 | v1.1 | Aligned with ISO 29147 — OWASP — CERT-In — NCSC UK
We welcome good-faith security research. We will not pursue legal action against researchers who comply with this policy.

1. Scope

  • Security vulnerabilities on darkpatternsolution.in
  • Privacy issues or data leaks on our website
  • Dark patterns on our own website (we take this seriously)
  • Dark patterns you've discovered in any Indian digital platform

2. Our Platform Self-Commitment

  • ✅ No pre-ticked checkboxes on our forms
  • ✅ No fake urgency or false scarcity messaging
  • ✅ All pricing transparent + GST stated clearly
  • ✅ Cookie consent with Accept + Reject buttons, equal prominence — no pre-accepted non-essential cookies
  • ✅ Quarterly self-audit of our own website

3. Response Timeline

  • Acknowledgement within 3 business days
  • Critical issues resolved within 30 days
  • Dark patterns on our site fixed within 14 days
  • Public disclosure after fix, or after 90 days if unresolved

4. How to Report

Security: [email protected]
Dark patterns (third-party): [email protected]
